ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee beverage class; no specific 2-in-1 coffee with creamer and no sugar subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKM covers instant coffee powder; no specific premixed creamer/no-sugar variant exists in FoodEx2. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT concept found for 2-in-1 instant coffee with creamer and no sugar as a food item.

allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain casein)

serving.description: 1 sachet prepared as directed (approximately 200 ml beverage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one sachet (approximately 12–14 g powder) dissolved in 180–200 ml of hot water, yielding roughly 200 g of prepared beverage.

nutrient_source.title: 2-in-1 Coffee (Milk, No Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for '2 in 1 coffee (milk, no sugar)', record ID D09010002. All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entry (Beverages, coffee, instant, with whitener, no sugar added; USDA SR Legacy #14214) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Instant Coffee with Non-Dairy Creamer (No Sugar) — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant coffee with whitener, no sugar
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for this specific HPB product. GI of 39 (low) estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant coffee with non-dairy creamer and no added sugar (closest analogous food). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (39) × available carbohydrate per serving (13.16 g per 200 g serving) / 100 = 5.1. Carbohydrate per serving = 6.58 g/100g × 200 g = 13.16 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium at 27 mg/100g; caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: With only 27 mg sodium per 100 g, this beverage does not contribute meaningfully to sodium load; however, individuals with hypertension should limit intake to 1–2 servings per day to avoid caffeine-mediated acute blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low sugar (2.83 g/100g), no added sugar, low estimated GI (~39); non-dairy creamer composition warrants label checking
type2_diabetes.guidance: The absence of added sugar and the low estimated GI (~39) make this a suitable beverage choice for people with Type 2 Diabetes; individuals should verify the creamer ingredient list to confirm the absence of maltodextrin or glucose syrup, which could raise the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~1.7 g/100g from non-dairy creamer, likely palm-oil based; low sodium and low calorie profile are favourable
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The non-dairy creamer in this product is likely palm-oil based, contributing an estimated 1.7 g saturated fat per 100 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to 1 serving per day and prefer formulations using unsaturated-fat creamers where available.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 2-in-1 Instant Coffee with Creamer (No Sugar) is a popular ready-to-dissolve beverage sachet widely consumed in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It consists of instant coffee granules pre-blended with a non-dairy creamer (typically palm-oil based), without any added sugar. The product is prepared by dissolving one sachet in 180–200 ml of hot water, producing a smooth, mildly bitter coffee beverage with a creamy mouthfeel. Per 100 g of prepared beverage, it provides 45 kcal, 0.52 g protein, 1.98 g fat, and 6.58 g carbohydrate, with naturally occurring sugars of 2.83 g and a low sodium content of 27 mg. Dietary fibre is negligible at 0.1 g. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 39 (low), making it a lower-glycemic beverage option compared to sweetened coffee mixes. Clinically, the low sodium and low sugar profile make it broadly suitable for individuals managing hypertension or Type 2 Diabetes, though the saturated fat contribution from the non-dairy creamer warrants moderation for those with cardiovascular disease. Coffee polyphenols (chlorogenic acids) present in the instant coffee component may confer mild anti-inflammatory benefits. This product is classified as ultra-processed due to the inclusion of non-dairy creamer and food additives. It is commonly consumed at breakfast and as a mid-morning beverage in Singapore, Malaysia, and the broader Southeast Asian region.
common_user_queries: Is 2-in-1 coffee without sugar good for diabetics?
How many calories in a sachet of 2-in-1 coffee no sugar?
Is instant coffee with creamer and no sugar suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of 2-in-1 coffee?
Does 2-in-1 coffee without sugar have saturated fat?
Can I drink 2-in-1 coffee if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (record D09010002), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food items. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous entry and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry and documented. All mandatory multilingual names for Singaporean cuisine are present.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.7 g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #14214 (Beverages, coffee, instant, with whitener, no sugar added) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item. This should be replaced with a label-verified value when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this specific instant coffee premix are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. 3. calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 per the HPB source (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database entry D09010002). 4. GI value of 39 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific HPB product was found. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical commercial formulations in Singapore; product-specific halal certification should be verified on the product label. 6. The non-dairy creamer component is likely palm-oil based, which contributes saturated fat and may have pro-inflammatory properties at high consumption levels.
